description_long: This object consists of two knives, each featuring a straight, pointed blade and a handle made from a light-colored, wood-like material. The blades are metallic, with one appearing slightly darker and more weathered than the other, suggesting different levels of use or finish. The handles are ergonomically shaped, tapering towards the blade and flaring at the end, with visible grain patterns and some carved or engraved details near the blade junction. One handle displays a distinct carved motif, adding a decorative touch. The knives are shown both sheathed and unsheathed, with the sheaths made from a similar wood or leather-like material, closely fitted to the blade. The overall design is utilitarian with subtle decorative elements, suitable for practical use or display.
description: A pair of straight-bladed knives with light wood handles, one featuring carved details and both accompanied by fitted sheaths. The blades are metallic, with one darker and more worn than the other.
description_view_0: Side view showing both knives unsheathed, highlighting the straight metallic blades and the light wood handles with carved details.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the sheaths covering the blades, emphasizing the fit and the material texture of the sheaths and handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view of the knives, one sheathed and one unsheathed, showing the blade profiles and the handle's ergonomic shape.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handles and the sheaths, with the blade tips partially visible and the carved motif on one handle more apparent.
attribute_handle_design: tapered, light wood, ergonomic with carved motif near blade
